description: A bell across the square may reach perception while someone is busy elsewhere. 'hea' reports that the sound was heard, though attention may have been elsewhere. 'sheluo' (listen) adds directed attention; 'hewasu' (audible) asks whether sound is available to a stated listener under the conditions at hand.
articulatory_notes: Let unvoiced air pass freely through the open glottis for 'h', then shape true-mid front 'e'. Keep the following open central 'a' as its own hiatus syllable. Stress falls on 'he'.
usage_notes: The source or content heard stands before the verb. Without an object, the verb can report hearing as an ability or event. In noun position, 'hea' is hearing.

semantic_domains.dialogue: spoken language becoming available to a hearer
